# Transcrição: Life Zones with Magali Peysha

- **Fonte:** Life Zones with Magali Peysha
- **Duração:** 15:40
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hi guys, I'm here today to talk to you about life zones strategy. So in the Goddess Project we have a session map and life zones session would come around somewhere around session 3, 4, it could be 5. You know this is a flexible outline. Life zones was created by Mark and I after studying six human needs for quite a while and thinking about what do we do at the start of the year or at the beginning of a coaching relationship to get quick understanding of all the parts of someone's life and to help them make goals that are not the type of goals like I want to lose 20 pounds or I want to get a raise or I want to take a vacation to Paris. Those goals are lovely but what about the deeper more meaningful goals that we always want for ourselves and for our clients of how we feel and so many times all of us are stuck in the idea that we can only really meet certain needs or have certain feelings in some areas of our life. So for example let's say that I'm a meditator and I believe that in meditation that's when I meet my need for relaxation and growth and connection with myself but during my work day I work at a busy restaurant everything's hectic and I can't meet that need at all. Now unfortunately as this person I have a problem. I only meditate half an hour a day and I meet my most important needs I experience the feelings I want most just during that half hour. This makes it a little hard to be happy at my job where things are chaotic and busy. So as this person's coach I want to help them make goals and have a plan for every life zone they're part of and if I know that their highest needs are met in their meditation practice now I can think ah how do I bring this in to that life zone of work that feels hectic and overwhelming and they're doing it about seven to nine hours a day so I know they need greater happiness in that work life zone. Okay so I have some steps for you about using life zones and I'm going to be consulting my outline over here. So step one ask your client to list out all their life zones. So for some people this could be easy and for some people this could be big. I definitely recommend if you're working in person or over Skype or

## [03:00 - 06:04]

however you do it that they have a big piece of paper and you might want to have a big pad. I use a big art pad for this kind of work. So we asked for all of the client's life zones and you tell them life zones are like for some people I jog that's one life zone. I'm a dog owner. I'm a mom. I'm a daughter who cares for her aging parents. I'm a receptionist for work. I'm a churchgoer. There would be six life zones right? So you get the idea and everybody has their own and some people might say well you know the relationship of being a friend is a really important life zone for me. So when you talk about social life you might say are there any relationships or activities you do that are really their own zone? Somebody else might have a hobby like bird watching. Now that's a big life zone for some people so you make that its own life zone. So life zones are unique to who your client is. There's no blueprint for everybody. This is unique to who you're working with and there's no right or wrong. Whatever your client tells you their life zones are that's great. So step two get curious just like in your discovery session get really curious about that life zone. Find out how your client feels when they're in that zone. Find out what they do who they're with. Do they have any patterns or triggers that they know of in that zone. Find out how long that zone has been part of their life. Have they always been a caregiver for example? Have they been jogging for 20 years? Did they own dogs as a child? You get the idea. Step three is ask your client what needs they get in that life zone. So now you've already had your six human needs sessions probably. If not you can explain what the needs are and you can ask what do you feel the most in that life zone? If they didn't know what the six human needs are you could say do you feel more love? Do you feel more certainty? Do you feel more uncertainty? Do you feel more growth? Do you feel more significance? Do you feel more contribution? And as and I said those out of order that as your client thinks about that and some for some in some life zones they might have all six in some life zones they might have only one. Now what I want you to keep in mind as a goal and especially today when you do a short practice session is that you're hoping to expand your clients needs in each life zone to be able to have at least two or three needs met. So let's say the only reason your client writes a blog is to feel important or that's the only reason they use social

## [06:04 - 09:09]

media. They don't actually like it but they feel that it's very important to to put themselves out there this way and gain respect so they meet their need for significance. You could reframe and elevate that zone by coming up with other ways that they could meet other needs. So for example could you help a friend or a child in your life create a blog the way you did and by doing that you're connecting and you're contributing. So now you get three needs met. Now this doesn't have to be a commitment from your client. This doesn't have to be a directive where they're going to show up and tell you they did this thing. Of course it could be if it sparked in them like yeah that's a good idea that's my goal. But it could just be by doing this in paper and having this map of all the life zones and the needs and possible ways to meet more needs in each zone that your client is starting to unconsciously create an idea for their life where they're more fulfilled in all these different ways in all those areas of life. And for some of us change happens rapidly and for some of us change happens slowly that's okay. Your client will take this experience and this growth and this learning and make per his own decisions about how to apply this strategy. You can help them but the responsibility isn't just on your shoulders. You've given them this incredible tool to see the life zones that they have and increase their ability to experience more to have more feelings to meet more needs to have more actions and experiences in each zone. So not just the needs what actions do they do in that zone what actions would they like to do. Now an action could be I run for a mile and I'd like to run two miles and action could be I want to run for a cause and action could be I want my children to run with me or my partner or my dog and action could be I want to carry my camera with me when I'm running so I can also take photographs or I bring my journal and at the end of my run I just write a paragraph about about how I feel or how I experience life. So actions are anything we do within that life zone and as a strategic interventionist you're going to become a great brainstormer if you aren't already. So I want you to brainstorm with your client what other actions they could do that would give them greater fulfillment and joy in each life zone. So you see this is growing into quite a map and it might take several pages and it might take several hours or several days. Now

Whenever in doubt as a strategic interventionist you can always use the research strategy so the research strategy is five minutes every day I'd like you to go online and research exciting things that you could do in your community with your family and just keep a list five minutes every day that's it now in those five minutes they're going to be meeting their need for variety at a very high level because they're envisioning and planning very fun things to do with their family of course next step is to do those things so wouldn't it be nice if you took your list that you make in that five minutes and once a month you plan get that planning done just once a month three activities in that month that you'll do with either your whole family or one member of your family that will feel very exciting and meet that need for variety so that's an example of how in the life zone of family we help the client experience easier ways to meet their needs through new vehicles for variety now we didn't have to go through all the needs we just went by what the client was saying about that life zone so you don't even need to do this step but my guess is as you're talking to a
